The ELM specifies a “Time Interval (Lookback Period)” of 190 mg/dL, which is a cholesterol concentration rather than a time duration. A lookback period should be expressed as a time interval (e.g., 12 months, 6 months). The age thresholds (≥30 years and ≤80 years) and the LDL Cholesterol value set are clinically reasonable.

The “Time Intervals (Lookback Periods)” field is incorrectly specified as “9 g%” (a value threshold) rather than a time interval (e.g., days, weeks, or months). The age threshold (≥ 18 years) and the value set identifier for HbA1c Laboratory Test are clinically reasonable.

The ELM implementation sets the age threshold to **≥30 years**, whereas current clinical practice guidelines recommend HIV screening for all adults **15–65 years** (or all adults regardless of age). This age restriction is therefore not clinically reasonable.
The value “35 kg/m²” is incorrectly listed under “Time Intervals (Lookback Periods)”; it is a BMI threshold, not a time interval.
Age threshold mismatch: The ELM implementation requires patients to be 25 years or older for alcohol misuse screening, whereas most clinical guidelines recommend screening adults 18 years and older.
